audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Pharaoh's paired dreams, Joseph's interpretation, seven years abundance/famine, Egyptian elevation, naming, Asenath, Manasseh/Ephraim, and famine administration.

verses.restored: At the end of two full years, Pharaoh dreamed that he was standing by the Nile.
Look, from the Nile came up seven cows, beautiful in appearance and fat in flesh, and they grazed among the reeds.
After them seven other cows came up from the Nile, ugly in appearance and thin in flesh, and stood beside the cows on the bank of the Nile.
The ugly and thin cows ate the seven beautiful and fat cows, and Pharaoh awoke.
He slept and dreamed a second time: seven ears of grain came up on one stalk, fat and good.
After them, seven thin ears, scorched by the east wind, sprouted.
The thin ears swallowed the seven fat and full ears. Pharaoh awoke, and look, it was a dream.
In the morning his spirit was troubled, and he called all Egypt’s magicians and wise men, but none could interpret the dreams for Pharaoh.
Then the chief cupbearer spoke to Pharaoh, saying, I remember my offenses today.
Pharaoh was angry with his servants and put me in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, me and the chief baker.
We dreamed a dream in one night, he and I, each according to the interpretation of his dream.
There was with us a Hebrew youth, servant of the captain of the guard; we recounted to him, and he interpreted our dreams to us.
As he interpreted to us, so it happened: me he restored to my office, and him he hanged.
Pharaoh sent and called Joseph, and they hurried him from the pit. He shaved, changed his garments, and came to Pharaoh.
Pharaoh said to Joseph, I have dreamed a dream, and there is no interpreter; I have heard it said of you that you hear a dream to interpret it.
Joseph answered Pharaoh, saying, It is not in me; the Divine will answer Pharaoh with peace.
Pharaoh told Joseph, In my dream I was standing on the bank of the Nile.
Seven cows came up from the Nile, fat in flesh and beautiful in form, and grazed among the reeds.
Then seven other cows came up after them, poor, very ugly in form, and thin in flesh; I had never seen such ugliness in all Egypt.
The thin and ugly cows ate the first seven fat cows.
When they had gone into them, it could not be known that they had gone into them, for their appearance was still ugly as at first. Then I awoke.
I saw in my dream, and look, seven ears came up on one stalk, full and good.
After them seven withered, thin ears, scorched by the east wind, sprouted.
The thin ears swallowed the seven good ears. I told it to the magicians, but there was no one who could explain it to me.
Joseph said to Pharaoh, Pharaoh’s dream is one; what the Divine is about to do, he has told Pharaoh.
The seven good cows are seven years, and the seven good ears are seven years; the dream is one.
The seven thin and ugly cows that came up after them are seven years, and the seven empty ears scorched by the east wind will be seven years of famine.
This is the thing I spoke to Pharaoh: what the Divine is about to do, he has shown Pharaoh.
Look, seven years are coming, great abundance in all the land of Egypt.
After them seven years of famine will arise, and all the abundance will be forgotten in the land of Egypt; the famine will consume the land.
The abundance will not be known in the land because of the famine afterward, for it will be very heavy.
The dream was doubled to Pharaoh because the thing is established by the Divine, and the Divine will hurry to do it.
Now let Pharaoh look for a discerning and wise man and set him over the land of Egypt.
Let Pharaoh appoint overseers over the land and take a fifth of the land of Egypt during the seven years of abundance.
Let them gather all the food of these good years that are coming, store grain under Pharaoh’s hand, and keep food in the cities.
The food shall be a reserve for the land for the seven years of famine that will be in Egypt, so the land will not be cut off by famine.
The word was good in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of all his servants.
Pharaoh said to his servants, Can we find a man like this, in whom is the spirit of the Divine?
Pharaoh said to Joseph, Since the Divine has shown you all this, there is none as discerning and wise as you.
You shall be over my house, and by your mouth all my people shall be ordered; only in the throne will I be greater than you.
Pharaoh said to Joseph, See, I have set you over all the land of Egypt.
Pharaoh removed his signet ring from his hand and put it on Joseph’s hand, clothed him in fine linen garments, and placed a gold chain on his neck.
He made him ride in the second chariot, and they called before him, Bow the knee. So he set him over all the land of Egypt.
Pharaoh said to Joseph, I am Pharaoh, but without you no one shall lift hand or foot in all the land of Egypt.
Pharaoh called Joseph’s name Zaphenath-paneah and gave him Asenath daughter of Potiphera priest of On as a woman. Joseph went out over the land of Egypt.
Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt. Joseph went out from Pharaoh’s presence and passed through all the land of Egypt.
In the seven years of abundance, the earth produced by handfuls.
He gathered all the food of the seven years in Egypt and put food in the cities, the food of the fields around each city he put within it.
Joseph stored grain like the sand of the sea, very much, until he stopped counting, for it was beyond number.
Before the year of famine came, two sons were born to Joseph, whom Asenath daughter of Potiphera priest of On bore to him.
Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh, for he said, The Divine has made me forget all my hardship and all my father’s house.
The name of the second he called Ephraim, for the Divine has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.
The seven years of abundance that were in the land of Egypt came to an end.
The seven years of famine began to come, as Joseph had said. There was famine in all lands, but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.
When all the land of Egypt was hungry, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread. Pharaoh said to all Egypt, Go to Joseph; whatever he says to you, do.
The famine was over all the face of the earth. Joseph opened all that was stored and sold to Egypt, and the famine was strong in the land of Egypt.
All the earth came to Egypt to Joseph to buy grain, because the famine was strong in all the earth.
